mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains near the effective lower bound, the equity discount rate stays compressed and S&P 500 prices re-rate upward over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rising policy rates do not weaken forward returns in the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower expected earnings, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The claim is falsified if unemployment spikes are followed by positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the 2-year rolling Rank IC is persistently above 0.03 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals broadening consumption and payroll growth, which supports revenue expectations and should lift the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales growth without payroll growth does not improve returns.
failure_condition: If joint-strength periods in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered joint states do not produce positive excess 20-day returns, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL is also accelerating, the market faces a growth-with-inflation mix that compresses margins and raises rate pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if output gains with stable prices do not outperform.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% m/m while INDPRO rises > 0.1% m/m
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term has no incremental explanatory power versus either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate regime, a further increase in FEDFUNDS tightens financial conditions and lowers equity present values, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days. The claim is falsified if rate hikes are not followed by weaker forward performance.
failure_condition: If periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the rolling Rank IC is positive for any 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and a broader cyclical slowdown that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if housing contraction is not followed by weaker equity performance within a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC stays near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in both jobs and unemployment terms, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid if labor weakness is not associated with weaker equity prices.
failure_condition: If joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE does not produce negative excess 20-day returns, or if the interaction model underperforms a naive zero-signal benchmark over any 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 indicates stronger real output and earnings capacity, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim fails if quarterly real GDP growth does not translate into a quarter-ahead equity gain.
failure_condition: Reject if higher GDPC1 growth is not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign of the relationship flips in more than one major rolling 3-year subperiod.
